title: Permutations of a Sequence
description: Given an array of nums containing n distinct integers, return all possible permutations of the array. You can return the answer in any order.

examples.explanation: The input array contains three distinct integers. The total number of permutations is 3! = 6. Each permutation is a unique arrangement of the numbers.
With two distinct integers, the possible arrangements are straightforward. There are 2! = 2 permutations: [0, 1] and [1, 0].
A single element has only one arrangement. Thus, the output is simply [[1]]. There are 1! = 1 permutation.

constraints: The input array nums must have at most 6 elements.
Each element of nums is a distinct integer.
hints: Consider using recursion to explore all arrangements of the elements.
Use a boolean array to track which elements are included in the current permutation.
Think about how to construct permutations by swapping elements in-place.
Remember to backtrack after exploring each permutation to restore the original state.
